title: Evangelion 20 OST Track List
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.0 OST Track List             Quote Post by The Triforce Fighter » Wed Jun 29, 2011 4:31 pm Hey guys, Basically I've managed to get my hands on a copy of Evangelion 2.0 OST thanks to my friend yet the names for the tracks on Disc one seem completely nonsensical. They seem to be a bunch of numbers and letters with a odd word thrown in for good measure here and there. Some tracks I can recognise myself like Misato's Theme yet other tracks new for the movie have left me stumped. If anyone can put me in the right direction I would appreciate it. Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jun 29, 2011 4:50 pm Do you mean something like this: Wikipedia wrote: Track listing: Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance Original Soundtrack (CD1) No. Title Lyrics Music Length 1. "2EM01_B01 At The Very Beginning" 2:37 2. "2EM03_0908" 0:59 3. "2EM04_E4" 1:42 4. "2EM05_KK_B09" 1:04 5. "2EM06_KK_B16" 0:08 6. "2EM07_KK_B09_InDoor" 1:04 7. "2EM08_B17" 0:43 8. "2EM09_YAMASHITA" Takayuki Inoue 1:21 9. "2EM10_KK_C01_AddGuit" 1:39 10. "2EM11_B16" 1:42 11. "2EM12_KK_A09" 1:35 12. "2EM13_EM20_Alterna" 1:56 13. "2EM14_EM10C" 1:06 14. "2EM15_0938 Destiny" 2:21 15. "2EM16_0944 Fate" 1:40 16. "2EM17_KK_A08" 1:35 17. "2EM18_KK_C01" 2:21 18. "2EM19_Sor: Variations on a Theme of Mozart" 1:00 19. "2EM20_KK_A09_Kuriya" 1:47 20. "2EM21_KK_C01_Str+AcGuit" 2:26 21. "2EM22_KK_A08_Orche" 2:07 22. "2EM25_Piano_Nu" 1:37 23. "2EM26_EM05B" 0:32 24. "2EM28_EM13" 0:51 25. "2EM29_E5" 2:00 26. "Today is the Time for Goodbye" (Performed by Megumi HayashibaraArrangement by Motoki Funayama & Shirō Sagisu) Shōichi Kaneko Shōichi Kaneko 2:43 27. "2EM31_0948 In My Spirit" 2:22 28. "2EM32_0910 Keep Your Head Above The Mayhem" 1:34 29. "2EM33_0902 The Final Decision We All Must Take" 3:29 30. "2EM34_E13" 2:00 31. "2EM35_Nu02 Carnage" 1:38 32. "2EM36_E16" 2:10 33. "Give Me Wings" (Performed by Megumi Hayashibara, Arrangement by Motoki Funayama & Shirō Sagisu) Michio Yamagami Kunihiko Murai 4:51 34. "2EM38_F02" 0:46 35. "Don't Turn Around" (Performed by The Peanuts) Tokiko Iwatani Hiroshi Miyagawa 2:55 36. "Season of Love" (Performed by Yōko Kon & Pinky & Killers) Tokiko Iwatani Taku Izumi 3:22 Total length: 65:28

[]:             Quote Post by The Triforce Fighter » Wed Jun 29, 2011 5:05 pm *sighs* So in other words there's no Sagisu to English translator available then = P I know I might seem picky but I do like my songs on my ipod to be easily recognised and I do not want to spend ages trying to work out which one is that cool sax track is. I guess I could always name them after the scenes themselves.... Just out of curiosity, do you know why Sagisu names his tracks in the way he does? Does it all make more sense in Japanese and its kinda lost in translation so to speak?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jun 29, 2011 5:05 pm KK = Karekano for those who didn't know. (Obviously, the Yamashita piece is from that film The Man Who Stole The Sun , but that was discussed elsewhere.)

[]:             Quote Post by The Triforce Fighter » Wed Jun 29, 2011 5:17 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: KK = Karekano for those who didn't know.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jun 29, 2011 5:22 pm http://myanimelist.net/anime/145/Kare_Kano An anime that Anno adapted from a manga (but was forced to quit) while Shiro Sagisu did the music for the series. Also, check out this thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=11202

[]:             Quote Post by The Triforce Fighter » Wed Jun 29, 2011 5:32 pm So some of my favourites tracks from 2.0 are actually from a totally different project altogether. Talk about cross referencing. The thread you linked to was an interesting read. Its nice to see Anno and Sagisu putting a great deal of effort into the OST's even if the majority of it is wasted on the Western World (Unless you do a lil bit of digging of course).

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Wed Jun 29, 2011 6:10 pm All I know is that 2EM means "Second Evangelion Movie". Other than that, I can't help you.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 29, 2011 6:27 pm The Triforce Fighter wrote: Just out of curiosity, do you know why Sagisu names his tracks in the way he does? Does it all make more sense in Japanese and its kinda lost in translation so to speak?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jun 29, 2011 8:51 pm Thank your for the detailed explanation, Reichu. And thanks for that link, SSD, I've translated some infos about Tsubasa wo Kudasai from the French wikipedia and submitted them in that thread.

[]:             Quote Post by The Triforce Fighter » Fri Jul 01, 2011 6:02 pm I see. Thanks for your help guys. Knew my fellow Eva Geeks would be able to help me out in my time of need = P

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sat Jul 02, 2011 10:57 pm Might not be too helpful but track 30 is called "Thanato's" and track 32 is called "The Beast II" or "Sin From Genesis". Both names work really. And track 25 is called "The Beast". That's all I got since I dont particulary care too much for the other tracks.
